## Figure Type
Geophysical image map (interpreted greyscale geophysical survey — likely aeromagnetic or radiometric data) with overlaid geological and mineral occurrence symbology. It functions as a mineral prospectivity map combining geophysical background imagery with tenement boundaries and known mineral occurrences.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers an area in the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, spanning approximately:
- **Eastings**: ~578,000 to 591,000 (MGA)
- **Northings**: ~6,973,000 to 6,991,000

Named locations visible include:
- **Eastmet Gossa** (VMS Prospect, upper right)
- **AM14** (VMS Prospect, upper centre)
- **Emily Well** (VMS Prospect, lower centre-right)

Tenement references visible:
- **E 20/742** (large Exploration Licence, outlined in blue)
- **P 20/2095**, **P 20/2096** (Prospecting Licences, blue outline, lower centre-left)
- **P 20/2302**, **P 20/2303** (Prospecting Licences, cyan outline, lower centre)

---

## Key Content

**Geophysical Background:**
The greyscale image shows strong linear to curvilinear structural fabric trending broadly **NNW–SSE**, consistent with Archean greenstone belt architecture typical of the Murchison Province. High-response (white/light) lineaments likely represent mafic/ultramafic lithologies or structural corridors, while dark zones may indicate felsic or less magnetic units. The prominent dark elongate body running north–south through the upper-right portion of the map is a strong geophysical contrast feature, possibly representing a large felsic volcanic unit or sedimentary horizon.

**Base Metal Horizons:**
Two prominent **red dashed lines** (base metal horizons) trend **NNW–SSE** through the map, running roughly parallel to the dominant structural grain. These pass through or adjacent to all three named VMS prospects (Eastmet Gossa, AM14, Emily Well), strongly implying a stratigraphic control on mineralisation.

**Mineral Occurrences:**
- **Gold occurrences** (yellow diamonds): Numerous, concentrated in a north–south corridor in the western portion of the map (within and adjacent to E 20/742 and the P licences). A strong cluster appears between northings ~6,976,000–6,980,000, coinciding with the western base metal horizon.
- **Base metal occurrences** (blue triangles): Sparse; two are visible near AM14 in the upper portion of the map, directly on the eastern base metal horizon.
- **VMS Prospects** (pink crosses): Three named prospects (Eastmet Gossa, AM14, Emily Well) all lie along or immediately adjacent to the base metal horizons.

**Tenement Layout:**
- E 20/742 is a large exploration licence occupying the central portion of the map.
- The four P-licences (P 20/2095, P 20/2096, P 20/2302, P 20/2303) form a tighter cluster in the southern part of E 20/742, centred on the gold occurrence cluster and proximal to the Emily Well VMS prospect.

## Research Value

This figure synthesises geophysical structural data with known mineral occurrences and tenement boundaries to demonstrate the **spatial relationship between base metal horizons (likely VMS-bearing stratigraphic units) and gold/base metal occurrences** across the project area. A geologist or AI research assistant would learn that mineralisation (both gold and base metals) is strongly controlled by two sub-parallel NNW-trending horizons, and that the concentration of prospecting licences in the south reflects a high-priority target area where multiple gold occurrences converge near the Emily Well VMS prospect. This figure directly supports an exploration model targeting **VMS and orogenic gold mineralisation** within a structurally complex Archean greenstone sequence in the Murchison.
